qa_pairs.question: What is the title of the webinar focused on managing large datasets in HPC?
qa_pairs.answer: The webinar is titled "Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC".

qa_pairs.question: Who provided the "Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: The webinar "Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC" was provided by Simon Fraser University (SFU) / West DRI.

qa_pairs.question: Who was the instructor for the "Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: Alex Razoumov was the instructor for the "Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC" webinar.

qa_pairs.question: When was the "Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC" webinar held?
qa_pairs.answer: The "Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC" webinar was held on Tuesday, May 20, 2025, at 10:00 AM PT.

qa_pairs.question: What topics were covered in the "Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: The webinar covered tools to enhance research workflows with large datasets, including in-situ visualization, lossy 3D data compression, distributed storage, and DAR (Disk ARchiver).

qa_pairs.question: What is in-situ visualization as discussed in the "Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: In-situ visualization enables interactive rendering of large in-memory arrays without the need to store them to disk.

qa_pairs.question: How effective is lossy 3D data compression, according to the "Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: Lossy 3D data compression can reduce the size of 3D datasets by up to 100X with no visible artifacts, making it ideal for storage and archival.

qa_pairs.question: What is DAR (Disk ARchiver) and its benefits?
qa_pairs.answer: DAR (Disk ARchiver) is described as a modern, high-performance alternative to TAR that offers indexing, differential archives, and faster extraction.

qa_pairs.question: Who is the target audience for the "Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: The webinar is for users working with large datasets.

qa_pairs.question: Where can one find recordings and materials for the "Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: Recordings and materials from the "Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC" webinar, and other related webinars, are freely available at https://training.westdri.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is the name of the online course offered by SHARCNET on GPU usage?
qa_pairs.answer: The online course offered by SHARCNET on GPU usage is titled "Mastering GPU Efficiency".

qa_pairs.question: Who instructs the "Mastering GPU Efficiency" course?
qa_pairs.answer: Sergey Mashchenko instructs the "Mastering GPU Efficiency" course.

qa_pairs.question: Is the "Mastering GPU Efficiency" course available at a specific time?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the "Mastering GPU Efficiency" course is available anytime as a self-paced online course.

qa_pairs.question: What is the main goal of the "Mastering GPU Efficiency" course?
qa_pairs.answer: The main goal of the "Mastering GPU Efficiency" course is to provide basic training for Alliance users on using GPUs on national systems, teaching them how to measure GPU utilization and improve it using NVIDIA technologies like MPS and MIG.

qa_pairs.question: Which NVIDIA technologies are covered in the "Mastering GPU Efficiency" course to improve GPU utilization?
qa_pairs.answer: The course covers NVIDIA technologies such as MPS (Multi-Process Service) and MIG (Multi-Instance GPU) to improve GPU utilization.

qa_pairs.question: Who should take the "Mastering GPU Efficiency" course?
qa_pairs.answer: Prospective users of the upgraded systems are the intended audience for the "Mastering GPU Efficiency" course.

qa_pairs.question: What is the format of the "Mastering GPU Efficiency" course and what is required to access it?
qa_pairs.answer: It is a 1-hour self-paced online course with a certificate of completion, and an Alliance CCDB account is required for access.

qa_pairs.question: What is the name of the webinar introducing SFU's newest cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The webinar introducing SFU's newest cluster is titled "Introduction to the Fir cluster".

qa_pairs.question: Which institution provided the "Introduction to the Fir cluster" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: Simon Fraser University (SFU) / West DRI provided the "Introduction to the Fir cluster" webinar.

qa_pairs.question: Who delivered the "Introduction to the Fir cluster" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: Alex Razoumov delivered the "Introduction to the Fir cluster" webinar.

qa_pairs.question: When was the "Introduction to the Fir cluster" webinar scheduled?
qa_pairs.answer: The "Introduction to the Fir cluster" webinar was scheduled for September 16, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What information was provided in the "Introduction to the Fir cluster" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: The webinar provided an overview of the Fir cluster, including its hardware, filesystems and recommended usage, job submission policies, and overall best practices for using the cluster.

qa_pairs.question: When did the Fir cluster become available?
qa_pairs.answer: The Fir cluster became available during August 2025.

qa_pairs.question: Who is the intended audience for the "Introduction to the Fir cluster" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: Prospective users of the Fir cluster are the intended audience.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find recordings and materials related to the "Introduction to the Fir cluster" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: Recordings and materials from previous related webinars are available at https://training.westdri.ca/getting-started/#high-performance-computing.

qa_pairs.question: What is the title of the SHARCNET webinar discussing upcoming GPU changes?
qa_pairs.answer: The SHARCNET webinar discussing upcoming GPU changes is titled "Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades".

qa_pairs.question: Who presented the "Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: Sergey Mashchenko presented the "Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades" webinar.

qa_pairs.question: When was the "Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades" webinar presented?
qa_pairs.answer: The "Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades" webinar was presented on Wednesday, November 20, 2024, from 12:00 PM to 1:00 PM ET.

qa_pairs.question: What are the significant changes to GPUs discussed in the "Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: The webinar discussed that older GPUs (P100, V100) will be replaced with H100 GPUs, increasing total GPU computing power by 3.5 times, but decreasing the number of GPUs from 3200 to 2100.

qa_pairs.question: What challenge will users face due to the GPU upgrades, as mentioned in the "Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: Users will face the challenge that the usual practice of using a whole GPU for each process or MPI rank will no longer be feasible in most cases due to the decreased number of GPUs.

qa_pairs.question: What NVIDIA technologies are recommended in the "Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades" webinar to mitigate GPU upgrade challenges?
qa_pairs.answer: NVIDIA technologies MPS (Multi-Process Service) and MIG (Multi-Instance GPU) are recommended to mitigate the challenges presented by the GPU upgrades.

qa_pairs.question: Who should attend the "Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: Prospective users of the upgraded systems, especially those intending to use a substantial amount of H100 resources (e.g., more than one GPU at a time, and/or over 24 hours runtime), should attend.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find the recording and slides for the "Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades" webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: The 1-hour presentation recording is available at https://youtu.be/pxY3G3BhwyA, and the slides are available at https://helpwiki.sharcnet.ca/wiki/images/1/1d/MIG_MPS.pdf.
